parameters.text:
=CONTEXT

You are an AI assistant that analyzes replies received via Lemlist on behalf of a company representative.

Your role is twofold:
1. Categorize the incoming reply
2. Draft an appropriate response aligned with the tone and business context


ABOUT THE COMPANY

{{ $('Config').item.json.companyName }} — {{ $('Config').item.json.companyContext }}

Default response language: {{ $('Config').item.json.defaultLanguage }}

STEP 1: ANALYSIS AND CATEGORIZATION

Analyze the reply and classify it into ONE of the following categories:
- meeting_request
- demo_information_request
- redirection
- not_interested


STEP 2: TONE ANALYSIS

Identify the tone of the reply:
- very_formal
- formal
- cordial


STEP 3: DRAFT RESPONSE

Write a response that:
- Matches the identified tone
- Is concise and clear
- Is action-oriented
- Directly addresses the intent of the reply

IMPORTANT RULES:

- For "meeting_request" and "demo_information_request":
  DO NOT suggest specific time slots.
  Use placeholders where time slots should appear.

- For "meeting_request":
  - Confirm availability
  - Include a placeholder
  - Suggest a meeting format (e.g., call, demo)

- For "demo_information_request":
  - Confirm that information will be shared
  - Offer a short call using a placeholder

- For "redirection":
  - Thank the contact
  - Ask for the appropriate person’s contact details

- For "not_interested":
  - Thank them politely
  - Keep the door open for future interaction


LEAD INFORMATION:

First Name: {{ $json.leadFirstName }}
Last Name: {{ $json.leadLastName }}
Company: {{ $json.leadCompanyName }}
Email Subject: {{ $json.subject }}
Campaign Name: {{ $json.campaignName }}


RECEIVED MESSAGE:

{{ $json.replyText || $json.replyPreview || 'Content not available' }}
=✅ *Answer sent to *{{ $('Parse Slack Interaction').item.json.leadEmail }}* via Lemlist (`{{ $('Parse Slack Interaction').item.json.category }}`)
=📝 *Managed manually* — *{{ $('Parse Slack Interaction').item.json.leadEmail }}* via Lemlist (`{{ $('Parse Slack Interaction').item.json.category }}`)

nodes.name: Config
nodes.notes: Per-team config. Duplicate this workflow per team and edit this node + Lemlist/Slack credentials.

parameters.content:
## Lemlist Reply AI

Lemlist reply → AI classifies & drafts → Slack Approve / Edit / Decline → reply sent via Lemlist.

**Do steps ① → ⑧ in order.** Workflow won't activate until every credential is set.

### ① Create Slack app

**Nodes to change:** none (external step in Slack)

**Do this:**
1. Open https://api.slack.com/apps in your browser.
2. Click **Create New App** (top-right) → **From a manifest**.
3. Pick your workspace → **Next**.
4. Delete everything in the YAML box, paste the provided manifest → **Next** → **Create**.
5. You land on the app's **Basic Information** page.
6. Left sidebar → **Install App** → click **Install to Workspace** → **Allow**.
7. Left sidebar → **OAuth & Permissions**.
8. Under **OAuth Tokens for Your Workspace**, copy the **Bot User OAuth Token** (starts with `xoxb-`). You'll paste it in step ②.

The placeholder Interactivity URL is fine for now; you'll replace it in step ⑦.

### ② Slack credential

**Nodes to change:**
• Slack Post Message (HTTP)
• Open Edit Modal
• Update Slack - Sent
• Update Slack - Manual
• Update Slack - Edited & Sent

**Do this:**
1. In n8n, top-left menu → **Credentials** → **Add credential**.
2. Search **Header Auth** → select **Header Auth**.
3. Fill:
   • Name (credential): `Slack Bot Token`
   • Name (header): `Authorization`
   • Value: `Bearer xoxb-…` (paste the token from step ①, keep the word `Bearer ` before it)
4. **Save**.
5. Open each of the 5 nodes above → in the node's **Credential** dropdown, pick `Slack Bot Token` → **Save** the node.

### ③ OpenAI credential

**Nodes to change:**
• OpenAI Chat Model
• OpenAI Chat Model1

**Do this:**
1. Open https://platform.openai.com/api-keys in your browser.
2. Click **Create new secret key** → copy it (starts with `sk-…`).
3. In n8n → **Credentials** → **Add credential** → search **OpenAI** → select **OpenAI API**.
4. Paste the key in **API Key** → **Save**.
5. Open both `OpenAI Chat Model` nodes → pick this credential → **Save**.

Default model `gpt-4o-mini` works. Swap to `gpt-4o` for higher quality.

### ④ Lemlist Basic Auth

**Nodes to change:**
• Send Reply via Lemlist
• Send Edited Reply via Lemlist

**Do this:**
1. In Lemlist (web app) → bottom-left profile icon → **Settings** → **Integrations** → **API**.
2. Copy your API key (click **Generate** first if none exists).
3. In n8n → **Credentials** → **Add credential** → search **HTTP Basic Auth** → select it.
4. Fill:
   • Name: `Lemlist API`
   • Username: your Lemlist team name (visible top-left in Lemlist)
   • Password: the API key
5. **Save**.
6. Open both nodes above → pick `Lemlist API` → **Save**.

### ⑤ Fill the Config node

**Nodes to change:**
• Config

**Do this — set these 4 fields inside the Config node:**
• **companyName** — your brand
• **companyContext** — what you do, for whom, value props, tone (3-5 lines)
• **slackChannelId** — in Slack, right-click the target channel → **View channel details** → scroll to bottom → copy the **Channel ID** (starts with `C…`)
• **defaultLanguage** — `en` or `fr`

Click **Save** in the node panel.

### ⑥ Activate the workflow

**Nodes to change:** none

**Do this:**
1. Top-right of the n8n editor → toggle **Active** (switch turns green).
2. If activation fails, n8n will highlight nodes in red — re-check credentials in ①–④.

Once active, both Webhook nodes now have **Production URLs** ready for steps ⑦ and ⑧.

### ⑦ Paste Interaction URL in Slack

**Nodes to change:**
• Slack Interaction Webhook (just copy its URL — no edits)

**Do this:**
1. In n8n, open the **Slack Interaction Webhook** node.
2. Click the **Production URL** field → copy the full URL.
3. In your Slack app (api.slack.com/apps → your app) → left sidebar → **Interactivity & Shortcuts**.
4. Toggle **Interactivity** on (if not already).
5. Paste the URL into **Request URL** → **Save Changes** (bottom-right).

nodes.name: ⑦ Paste Interaction URL in Slack

parameters.content:
### ⑧ Register webhook in Lemlist

**Nodes to change:**
• Webhook (top of Flow A — just copy its URL)

**Do this:**
1. In n8n, open the **Webhook** node at the very start of the flow.
2. Copy its **Production URL**.
3. In Lemlist → **Settings** → **Integrations** → **Webhooks** → **+ Add webhook**.
4. Paste the URL.
5. Event: select `lemlistReplyReceived`.
6. **Save**.

Send a test reply to a live Lemlist campaign to verify the Slack message appears.

### Optional: tune the AI prompt

**Nodes to change (only if customizing):**
• AI Agent
• Structured Output Parser (keep schema in sync if you add fields)

**Do this (optional):**
Company info is already injected from the Config node — no edit needed to ship.

To customize, open AI Agent and adjust the categories (`meeting_request`, `demo_information_request`, `redirection`, `not_interested`) and the reply rules per category.
